title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3,000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
summary: Global aluminum prices have surged past $3,000 per tonne on the London Metal Exchange due to structural supply deficits, China's production caps, and surging demand from EV and renewable sectors. This marks a multi-year peak, benefiting low-carbon producers like Alcoa and Rio Tinto while raising inflationary fears for downstream industries. The rally is driven by geopolitical shifts and carbon-border taxes in the West.

title: Aluminium Prices Soar as Tariffs and Energy Costs Reshape the Market
summary: Aluminum prices have hit four-year highs, with LME futures exceeding April 2022 levels and U.S. premiums surpassing $1 per pound, fueled by high energy costs, tariffs, and trade disputes. Goldman Sachs forecasts an average $3,150/ton in H1 2026, benefiting producers like Alcoa and Century Aluminum but pressuring downstream sectors such as automotive and packaging. Trump's 50% tariffs on aluminum from summer 2025 contribute to this new market phase.

title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
summary: Aluminum prices have surged past $3,000 per tonne due to a structural supply deficit from China's production caps, geopolitical instability, and surging demand from EV and renewable sectors. The market is shifting towards 'green' aluminum with increased focus on recycling to meet energy transition needs. Producers like Rio Tinto and Norsk Hydro benefit while downstream industries face high costs.

title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs amid fears of prolonged Middle East shipping disruptions, including closure of the Strait of Hormuz affecting regional exports. Additional supply concerns arise from South32's Mozal smelter in Mozambique going offline due to power issues. This has flipped market contango into a premium, with low inventories exacerbating tightness.

title: IBAAS–2026 to Spotlight Innovation, Sustainability and Digital Transformation in the Global Aluminium Industry
summary: The IBAAS 2026 conference will unite global aluminium experts in India with 10 specialized technical sessions covering bauxite innovation, recycling, green aluminium, AI applications, and sustainable practices. Key focus areas include cathode technology innovations, advanced aluminium alloys, circular economy initiatives, and emerging technologies like artificial intelligence in aluminium operations and decarbonization strategies.

title: Green grant awarded: Advancing circular aluminium for sustainable water treatment
summary: Alumichem has received a green grant to develop a circular production process for aluminium chlorohydrate (ACH) by replacing virgin aluminium with recycled aluminium. The project, supported by Denmark's Environmental Technology Development and Demonstration Programme, aims to reduce energy use, CO₂ emissions, and material waste while maintaining high quality for drinking water applications.

title: Next-Generation Smelting Technology for Sustainable Production
summary: Century Aluminum and Emirates Global Aluminum (EGA) are partnering to build a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma using next-generation EX smelting technology designed to improve productivity and reduce energy consumption and emissions. The facility is expected to produce up to 750,000 metric tons annually, with construction beginning as early as 2026 and commercial production anticipated before the end of the decade.

title: The Aluminum Renaissance: A Deep-Dive into Alcoa's (AA) 2026 Strategic Pivot
summary: Alcoa is advancing its Sustana line of low-carbon products, including EcoSource (the world's first low-carbon alumina) and EcoLum (aluminum with less than 4.0 kg CO₂e per kg). The company's ELYSIS joint venture with Rio Tinto has moved into extended validation phase with carbon-free smelting technology that emits pure oxygen instead of CO₂, positioning Alcoa as a future technology licensor for the global smelting industry.

title: Aluminium Industry in 2026: Global Trends, Innovations, and What Buyers Should Expect
summary: The aluminum industry in 2026 is increasingly focused on sustainability, with 100 percent recyclability and circular economy principles becoming central to procurement decisions. Leading manufacturers are adopting AI-enabled process monitoring, real-time optimization, and predictive maintenance systems to reduce production variability and improve quality while meeting ESG and sustainability frameworks.

title: Aluminum at the Centre of Global Industrial Transformation AICE 2026
summary: AICE 2026 highlights how recycling and circularity are moving to the forefront of the aluminum industry, with recycled aluminum requiring a fraction of the energy needed for primary production. Regulatory pressure and corporate sustainability commitments are accelerating investment in scrap collection, sorting, and remelting technologies, with closed-loop systems being developed for construction, automotive, and packaging applications.

title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
summary: The aluminum scrap industry is undergoing a circular economy revolution with companies investing in closed-loop recycling, green smelting technologies, and advanced sorting systems. Demand for recycled aluminum is booming in Asia-Pacific, with China sourcing about 60% of its building-sector aluminum from recycled materials and projections showing a 40% increase in construction output by 2030.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel prices rose to 3,148 CNY/T on March 24, 2026, up 0.13% from the previous day, with a 2.54% increase over the past month despite being 2.24% lower than a year ago. Steel rebar futures climbed to around CNY 3,150 per ton, nearing highest levels since August last year, as China curbs overcapacity amid weak demand. Higher raw material costs from rising ocean freight and energy prices due to the Iran war are squeezing steel mill margins, prompting production cuts.

title: Metal prices on March 24, 2026: Precious metals experience significant correction, steel prices maintain upward trend
summary: Steel prices maintained an upward trend on March 24, 2026, while precious metals saw significant corrections. This reflects ongoing positive momentum in the steel market amid broader metal price adjustments.

title: Metal Logic Purchases Pilbara Site to Deploy Grade-Agnostic, Scalable Modular Clean Steel Smelter
source: Steel Industry Press Releases | Global Steel Updates
summary: Low-carbon steel platform Metal Logic has acquired approximately 1,000 hectares of tenements in Western Australia's Pilbara region, strategically positioned within 20 kilometres of major rail corridors operated by BHP, Roy Hill, and Fortescue. The location provides access to rail infrastructure that collectively transports more than half the iron ore mined in the Pilbara to the coast for export.

title: Small scale demolitions and piling work start at EAF sites
source: Steel Industry Press Releases | Global Steel Updates
summary: Tata Steel UK continues its decarbonization transformation at the Port Talbot site, with ongoing demolition of redundant assets and groundworks preparation for new construction projects. This work is part of the company's broader effort to secure the long-term future of steel production in South Wales.

title: Novelis Wins Award for 100% Recycled End-Of-Life Vehicle Scrap Product
source: Steel Industry Press Releases | Global Steel Updates
summary: Novelis Inc. received the Münchner Management Kolloquium (MMK) Award of Excellence 2026 for its aluminium sheet made with 100% end-of-life vehicle scrap, designed for exterior car body applications. The award recognizes the company's innovation in sustainable progress through recycled material solutions.

title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: US copper prices are expected to remain volatile in 2026 due to factors like Chinese demand, supply constraints, and AI sector growth. Goldman Sachs forecasts an average of $5.17/lb in 2026, up from $4.82/lb in 2025, with a small market surplus. Other analysts like Citigroup predict higher prices above $5.90/lb by Q2 2026.

title: Three Forces Shaping Copper's Path Forward
summary: Copper prices rallied strongly into 2026 after a 41% gain in 2025, driven by AI data centers, EVs, grid modernization, and supply shortages. Consensus forecasts range from $5.00-$6.35/lb for 2026, though Goldman Sachs sees a 160,000-ton surplus pressuring prices. J.P. Morgan projects a 330,000-ton refined copper deficit amid low inventories.

title: Copper Prices Jump 2% Today, After Hitting a Record High in Jan 2026
summary: Copper prices jumped 2% on the day of reporting after reaching a record high in January 2026, amid a forecasted 15-year global supply shortage. Existing supply constraints are keeping prices elevated, pressuring margins for cables and wires companies where copper accounts for 65-67% of costs. Analysts highlight volatility risks and weak domestic demand.

title: Copper Prices Are Forecast to Decline Somewhat from Record Highs in 2026
summary: Goldman Sachs expects copper prices to decline to $10,000-$11,000 per tonne in 2026 from record highs, due to a global surplus of 160kt, preventing sustained prices above $11,000. Chinese demand fell -8% y/y in Q4 2025, but longer-term structural demand from power infrastructure could lift prices beyond 2026. Potential US tariffs on refined copper imports by mid-2026 may boost near-term flows.

title: Copper Prices Surge to Record Highs
summary: Comex copper futures hit $6.58/lb on Jan 29, 2026, up 7% that day and the highest since 1972, driven by Chinese buying and a softer dollar. Analysts are split: Goldman Sachs sees a surplus and prices easing to $11,000/ton by year-end, while J.P. Morgan forecasts a 330,000-ton deficit and peaks near $5.67/lb. Risks include economic slowdowns, new supply, and US tariff decisions by mid-2026.

title: Copper for Tomorrow: Sustainable copper production
summary: The Copper for Tomorrow bid seeks to address challenges in the copper value chain to boost sustainable production amid forecasts of supply shortfalls essential for clean energy transitions. It focuses on processing lower-grade ores with less energy, water, and waste, while electrifying mining operations. The initiative unites industry and researchers to meet ESG goals and increase profitable output from complex ore bodies.

title: Zero Emission Copper Mine of the Future – Material Movement Report
summary: This report outlines technologies for zero-emission copper mining, emphasizing electrification of vehicles, renewable energy integration, and reduced materials movement through efficient conveying and ore sorting. It covers current (Horizon 1), near-term (Horizon 2), and future (Horizon 3) solutions tailored to unique mine sites. The goal is to help operators create site-specific 'Roadmap to Zero' for emissions reduction amid rising ESG pressures.

title: Ten major mining tech trends in 2026: Part 1
summary: The article highlights innovations like reprocessing copper waste stockpiles, with Freeport-McMoRan targeting 360,000 tpa from chalcopyrite and Amerigo generating revenue from El Teniente mine waste. Atlantic Copper's CirCular project aims to recover multiple metals from e-waste. It also discusses reducing water use in copper processing by up to 78% through innovative flowsheets, urging a shift in valuing water like energy and carbon.

title: METALMARKETUPDATE - MARCH 2026
summary: Nickel futures fell to around $7.801 per pound in early March amid heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, including Iran's threat to close the Strait of Hormuz, which drove oil prices higher and strengthened the dollar. Market participants remain cautious due to risk-off flows in industrial metals, while supply-side factors like Indonesia's 2026 ore quota and new project ramp-ups limit tightness. U.S. tariff policies are in flux following a Supreme Court ruling, creating uncertainty in global metals markets.

title: SMM Nickel Midday Review: Nickel Prices Rebounded Sharply on March 25 as the US Was Considering Pushing for a One-Month C
summary: On March 25, the SMM price of #1 refined nickel rose by 1,650 yuan/mt from the previous trading day. Spot premiums adjusted accordingly amid the rebound. The price surge is linked to US considerations for a one-month policy push.

title: Indonesian nickel firms to re-apply for 2026-27 permits
summary: Indonesian nickel mining firms are required to re-apply for their RKAB production permits for 2026-27 between 1 October and 15 November, while 2025 approvals remain unchanged. This regulatory update aims to manage nickel output amid global market dynamics. The move coincides with broader challenges in battery metals mining due to diesel supply disruptions.

title: Asia's EVolution: No clear skies in Indonesia's nickel mining towns
summary: The article examines environmental and social challenges in Indonesia's nickel mining towns amid the country's push into EV battery production. Despite rapid expansion in nickel output, local communities face pollution and health issues from mining operations. It highlights the tensions between global demand for nickel and on-the-ground impacts.

title: Nickel Industries showcases sustainability in quarterly update
summary: Nickel Industries Limited reported stable operations and a perfect safety record with zero lost-time injuries and 17.7 million safe man-hours in its Q4 2025 quarterly update. The company advanced sustainability through a strategic acquisition of a 10% stake in Excelsior Nickel Cobalt and received awards for ESG leadership. This highlights their focus on workforce safety and integration into high-quality global supply chains.

title: Inside a $1.1B deal to reshore critical minerals refining
summary: Nth Cycle secured a $1.1 billion deal with Trafigura to expand its electrochemical refining system for nickel, cobalt, copper, and other critical minerals from battery scrap. The compact technology reduces size and costs compared to traditional refineries, addressing U.S. and Europe's reliance on Indonesia and China for nickel processing. This innovation supports reshoring by recycling domestic battery waste instead of exporting it.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Nickel prices reached $18,000 in 2026 amid oversupply and EV demand growth, with battery-grade nickel demand projected to triple by 2030. Alaska Energy Metals' Nikolai Project advances as a key U.S. sulphide nickel resource, gaining FAST-41 status to accelerate permitting and infrastructure for domestic supply security. This aligns with efforts to reduce foreign reliance for clean energy transitions.

title: Tenaris to supply line pipe, OCTG, coating and services for Sakarya Phase 3 in offshore Turkey
summary: Tenaris has been awarded a contract to supply line pipe, casing, coating and related services for the third phase of the Sakarya gas field project in offshore Turkey. The contract includes delivery of 22,700 tons of seamless pipes from its Dalmine mill in Italy and 87,800 tons of welded pipes from its Confab mill in Brazil, with comprehensive TenarisShawcor coating services.

title: From production to installation: Tenaris brings integrated solutions to MCE 2026
summary: Tenaris is showcasing its portfolio of hot-rolled seamless pipes for civil and industrial applications at Mostra Convegno Expocomfort 2026 in Milan, Italy (March 24-27). The company highlights its integrated European supply chain, including the Piombino production facility in Italy, and recently certified environmentally sustainable pipe products.

title: Tenaris ends $600M buyback tranche after $583.6M spent
summary: Tenaris terminated the second tranche of its USD 600 million share buyback program effective March 3, 2026, after repurchasing approximately USD 583.6 million worth of shares. The company cited market volatility and potential incremental payout mechanics as reasons for the early termination.

title: Prysmian Creates World's First Negative-Carbon-Footprint Cable
summary: Prysmian has created the world's first cable with a negative carbon footprint, representing a significant innovation in sustainable cable manufacturing. This development aligns with the company's commitment to advancing environmentally responsible energy infrastructure solutions.

title: Prysmian signs €2.3 billion contract for UK power link project
summary: Italian cable maker Prysmian has signed a €2.3 billion contract with SP Energy Networks and National Grid to build the Eastern Green Link 4, a power link connecting the electrical grids of Scotland and England. This represents a significant infrastructure project for the company specializing in energy and telecom cable systems.
